tags.label: nature

tags.label: memory
tags.description: Domain axis memory: a stable framing category used to connect series by shared discourse. Indicates context (science, memory, language, nature) without requiring literal depiction of the term.

tags.label: signal
tags.description: Communications/information framing: signal, channel, noise, encoding, and detection. Emphasises legibility and measurement metaphors; contrasts with purely expressive mark-making or purely representational depiction.

tags.label: language

tags.label: line
tags.description: line as compositional structure: describes how marks or elements behave (directionality, clustering, enclosure, repetition). Provides geometry-based similarity even when depicted subjects differ.

tags.label: curvilinear
tags.description: Formal quality curvilinear: a structural descriptor affecting perception of density, flow, boundary, or tone. Often scale-dependent (micro texture vs macro composition); distinct from interpretive themes.

tags.label: radial
tags.description: Organisation around a centre with spokes, arcs, or concentric tendencies. Produces rotational balance and outward dispersion; common in blooms, bursts, vortices; distinct from linear flow.

tags.label: dense
tags.description: High mark or element density with limited negative space. Reads as compression, pressure, or accumulation; can increase cognitive load and visual noise; distinct from sparse, airy distributions.

tags.label: sparse
tags.description: Low element density with ample negative space. Reads as openness, pause, or isolation; emphasises placement and interval; distinct from dense, crowded, or saturated surfaces.

tags.label: enclosed
tags.description: Formal quality enclosed: a structural descriptor affecting perception of density, flow, boundary, or tone. Often scale-dependent (micro texture vs macro composition); distinct from interpretive themes.

tags.label: layered
tags.description: Multiple strata or passes remain visible: palimpsest, accretion, or composite overlays. Encodes time and revision; can be transparent or opaque; distinct from single-pass, flat surfaces.

tags.label: fragmented
tags.description: Broken into discontinuous parts: gaps, cuts, shards, or interrupted contours. Reads as interruption or assembly; can be collage-like or glitch-like; distinct from continuous, flowing forms.

tags.label: growth
tags.description: Theme growth: an inferred emphasis that helps articulate patterns across a series—tension, drift, emergence, repair. Interpretive and revisable; distinct from purely formal descriptors.

tags.label: repetition
tags.description: repetition as motif thread: a conceptual/experiential reading that recurs across works. Useful for writing and clustering; keep separate from domain framing and subject depiction to avoid category drift.

tags.label: depth
tags.description: Formal quality depth: a structural descriptor affecting perception of density, flow, boundary, or tone. Often scale-dependent (micro texture vs macro composition); distinct from interpretive themes.

tags.label: shallow
tags.description: shallow as compositional structure: describes how marks or elements behave (directionality, clustering, enclosure, repetition). Provides geometry-based similarity even when depicted subjects differ.

tags.label: sharp
tags.description: Edges and local contrast are crisp; high spatial-frequency detail is preserved. Seen in hard ink lines, high-acutance photos, or clean vector forms; distinct from blur, haze, and soft focus.

tags.label: blurred
tags.description: Edges lose high-frequency detail through motion, depth-of-field, diffusion, or averaging. Produces softness and ambiguity; distinct from low-resolution pixelation, grain, or deliberate smearing.

tags.label: grainy
tags.description: High-frequency particulate texture: many small intensity variations at short spatial scales. Appears as film grain, high-ISO sensor noise, dithering, or sand-like stipple; distinct from smooth gradients.

tags.label: smooth
tags.description: Low micro-variation: gradients or planes read continuous, with minimal particulate texture. Seen in clean ink washes, polished surfaces, or compressed images; distinct from grainy, gritty, or speckled fields.

tags.label: textured
tags.description: Surface with tactile variation—roughness, grain, or patterned micro-structure. Can be paper tooth, corrosion, stipple, or fabric weave; distinct from flat, uniform tone and smooth surfaces.

tags.label: minimal
tags.description: Formal quality minimal: a structural descriptor affecting perception of density, flow, boundary, or tone. Often scale-dependent (micro texture vs macro composition); distinct from interpretive themes.

tags.label: maximal
tags.description: Formal quality maximal: a structural descriptor affecting perception of density, flow, boundary, or tone. Often scale-dependent (micro texture vs macro composition); distinct from interpretive themes.

tags.label: monochrome
tags.description: Single-hue or near-black-and-white palette emphasising value, texture, and structure over colour relationships. Common in ink and B/W photography; distinct from polychrome or colour-coded layers.

tags.label: limited-palette
tags.description: limited palette as formal property: describes organisation, texture, contrast, rhythm, or spatial behaviour. Typically observable in distribution and edge quality; contrasts with subject motifs and conceptual domains.

tags.label: polychrome
tags.description: Formal quality polychrome: a structural descriptor affecting perception of density, flow, boundary, or tone. Often scale-dependent (micro texture vs macro composition); distinct from interpretive themes.

tags.label: glitch
tags.description: Digital or process error aesthetic: discontinuities, tearing, blocks, misregistration, or codec artifacts. Reads as system failure or mediation; distinct from organic irregularity and hand jitter.

tags.label: overlay
tags.description: Two or more layers occupy the same field, partially occluding or blending. Can be double exposure, composite drawing passes, or UI superposition; distinct from simple repetition without stacking.

tags.label: becoming
tags.description: becoming as motif thread: a conceptual/experiential reading that recurs across works. Useful for writing and clustering; keep separate from domain framing and subject depiction to avoid category drift.

tags.label: change
tags.description: change as interpretive motif: a recurring idea or experienced quality inferred from the work. Not a factual claim about intent; contrasts with domain context and with visible subject motifs.

tags.label: transformation

tags.label: signal
tags.description: Motif of legibility versus interference: what stands out, what is obscured, and how meaning survives noise. Often expressed as partial messages, traces, thresholds; contrasts with pure chaos or silence.

tags.label: noise
tags.description: Motif of interference, excess, and irreducible variation. Reads as ambiguity, friction, or entropy; can be comforting or oppressive; contrasts with clarity, order, and clean transmission.

tags.label: calm
